title: encelia glandulosa
intro: Encelia glandulosa, also known as the brittlebrush or brittle bush, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that may help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Encelia glandulosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
